definition: The use of technology to perform tasks with where human input is minimized.
The theoretical ability of an AI to understand, learn, and apply knowledge across a wide range of tasks at a human level.
An interactive experience where real-world environments are enhanced by computer-generated perceptual information.
A simulated 3D environment that users can explore and interact with.
The merging of real and virtual worlds to produce new environments where physical and digital objects co-exist.
An umbrella term encapsulating Augmented Reality, Virtual Reality, and Mixed Reality.
A collective virtual shared space, created by the convergence of virtually enhanced physical reality and persistent virtual spaces.
A virtual representation that serves as the real-time digital counterpart of a physical object or process.
The network of physical objects embedded with sensors and software for connecting and exchanging data.
A distributed computing paradigm that brings computation and data storage closer to the location where it is needed.
The use of quantum phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation.
The fifth generation of technology standard for broadband cellular networks.
A growing list of records, called blocks, that are linked using cryptography.
A computer program or a transaction protocol which automatically executes, controls or documents legally relevant events.
An organization represented by rules encoded as a computer program that is transparent and controlled by members.
An idea for a new iteration of the World Wide Web which incorporates concepts such as decentralization, blockchain technologies, and token-based economics.
Digital labor platforms that facilitate short-term, flexible employment arrangements.
Established digital workflows and guidelines enabling teams to collaborate effectively from distributed locations.
A set of technologies which allow a person to feel as if they were present, to give the appearance of being present.
An application program that understands natural language voice commands and completes tasks for the user.
The combination of RPA with advanced cognitive technologies such as machine learning and NLP.
A form of business process automation technology based on metaphorical software robots.
A development environment that provides a graphical user interface to build applications with minimal coding.
A platform allowing non-technical users to build software applications without writing code.
Techniques designed to help determine the condition of in-service equipment to estimate when maintenance should be performed.
The on-demand availability of computer system resources, especially data storage and computing power.
A direct communication pathway between an enhanced or wired brain and an external device.
